<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Create Lexical-to-Markdown converter with wiki link preservation</name>
  <files>src/utils/markdownExport.ts</files>
  <action>
    Create markdown conversion utility for notes:

    1. **Install @lexical/markdown package:**
       ```bash
       npm install @lexical/markdown
       ```

    2. **Create src/utils/markdownExport.ts:**

       **exportNoteToMarkdown(note: Note, allNotes: Record<string, Note>): string**
       - Parse Lexical JSON content from note.content
       - Convert Lexical nodes to markdown using @lexical/markdown transformers
       - Preserve wiki links: [[Note Title]] syntax (already in content)
       - Add frontmatter header:
         ```markdown
         ---
         title: Note Title
         created: 2025-12-01T12:00:00Z
         updated: 2025-12-01T14:30:00Z
         tags: [tag1, tag2]
         folder: Folder Name (if folderId exists)
         pinned: true/false
         favorite: true/false
         ---
         ```
       - Resolve wiki links to valid note titles (check allNotes for existence)
       - Convert Lexical formatting: bold, italic, code, lists, headings, links, code blocks
       - Handle embedded images (convert to markdown image syntax if data URLs)
       - Strip Lexical-specific metadata (paragraph IDs, etc.)

       **getMarkdownFilename(note: Note): string**
       - Sanitize title for filesystem (remove special characters: <, >, :, ", /, \, |, ?, *)
       - Replace spaces with hyphens or underscores
       - Add .md extension
       - Truncate to 255 characters max (filesystem limit)
       - Example: "My Note Title!" → "my-note-title.md"

       **isValidMarkdown(markdown: string): boolean**
       - Basic validation: non-empty, has content beyond frontmatter
       - Check frontmatter parses correctly (YAML format)
       - Return true if valid, false otherwise

    3. **Lexical transformers to use:**
       - HEADING transformer (h1-h6)
       - BOLD transformer (**bold**)
       - ITALIC transformer (*italic*)
       - CODE transformer (`code`)
       - LINK transformer ([text](url))
       - LIST transformers (ordered/unordered)
       - CODE_BLOCK transformer (```language```)
       - QUOTE transformer (> quote)

    4. **Pattern to follow:** src/utils/backlinks.ts (similar note content parsing)

    5. **Avoid:**
       - Losing wiki links during conversion (preserve [[Note Title]] syntax exactly)
       - Creating invalid filenames (sanitize thoroughly)
       - Corrupting Lexical JSON (parse safely with try/catch)

    6. **Why:** Lexical stores rich text as JSON - need reliable conversion to portable markdown format. Wiki links are core feature that must survive export.

    Research: https://lexical.dev/docs/concepts/serialization, https://www.npmjs.com/package/@lexical/markdown
  </action>
  <verify>
    - npm list @lexical/markdown shows installed version
    - exportNoteToMarkdown() converts sample note with headings, bold, lists to valid markdown
    - Wiki links [[Note Title]] preserved in output
    - Frontmatter YAML parses correctly (tags array, dates ISO format)
    - getMarkdownFilename() sanitizes special characters correctly
    - isValidMarkdown() validates frontmatter format
  </verify>
  <done>Lexical-to-markdown converter working, wiki links preserved, frontmatter formatted, filename sanitization robust</done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Create task-to-markdown formatter with metadata</name>
  <files>src/utils/markdownExport.ts</files>
  <action>
    Add task export functionality to markdownExport.ts:

    1. **exportTaskToMarkdown(task: Task): string**
       - Format task as markdown with frontmatter:
         ```markdown
         ---
         title: Task Title
         status: todo/inprogress/done
         priority: low/medium/high
         created: 2025-12-01T12:00:00Z
         start-date: 2025-12-02 (YYYY-MM-DD or null)
         due-date: 2025-12-15 (YYYY-MM-DD or null)
         tags: [tag1, tag2]
         card-number: KAN-42 (if exists)
         ---

         ## Description

         [task description - markdown formatted]

         ## Checklist

         - [ ] Checklist item 1
         - [x] Completed item 2

         ## Metadata

         - **Status:** Todo
         - **Priority:** High
         - **Created:** December 1, 2025
         - **Due Date:** December 15, 2025
         - **Estimated Hours:** 8 (if exists)
         - **Actual Hours:** 5 (if exists)
         - **Progress:** 60% (if exists)
         ```

    2. **Include optional fields conditionally:**
       - Dependencies: list as markdown list with task IDs
       - Subtasks: nested checklist items
       - Comments: as separate section with timestamps
       - Recurrence: formatted as human-readable ("Every 2 weeks on Monday")
       - Time tracking: duration breakdown (if exists)
       - Attachments: list with filenames (note: data URLs not exportable to MD, warn user)

    3. **exportTasksToMarkdown(tasks: Task[]): string**
       - Bulk export format: single markdown file with all tasks
       - Group by status (Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Review, Done)
       - Each task as h2 heading with metadata below
       - Table of contents at top linking to tasks

    4. **Format dates:**
       - Use human-readable format: "December 1, 2025" in body
       - Use YYYY-MM-DD in frontmatter (machine-readable)
       - Handle null dates gracefully ("No due date")

    5. **Pattern to follow:** Existing note templates in useNotesStore.ts (similar markdown formatting)

    6. **Avoid:**
       - Exporting sensitive data without user knowledge (all fields opt-in)
       - Invalid YAML in frontmatter (quote strings with special chars)
       - Losing task structure (preserve hierarchy with indentation)

    7. **Why:** Tasks have rich metadata (dates, priorities, dependencies) that must be preserved in export. Markdown is universal format for task managers.

    Research: https://github.github.com/gfm/ (GitHub Flavored Markdown spec)
  </action>
  <verify>
    - exportTaskToMarkdown() formats task with frontmatter correctly
    - Checklist items preserved with [ ] and [x] syntax
    - Optional fields (dependencies, comments, recurrence) included conditionally
    - exportTasksToMarkdown() groups tasks by status
    - Date formatting consistent (ISO in frontmatter, human-readable in body)
    - YAML frontmatter validates in online YAML parser
  </verify>
  <done>Task-to-markdown formatter complete, metadata preserved, bulk export working, optional fields handled</done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 3: Create file system export helpers and folder structure utilities</name>
  <files>src/utils/markdownExport.ts</files>
  <action>
    Add file system utilities for folder-structure export:

    1. **createMarkdownZip(files: Array<{path: string, content: string}>): Blob**
       - Use JSZip library to create ZIP archive
       - Install: `npm install jszip`
       - Each file: { path: "folder/subfolder/note.md", content: "# Note..." }
       - Preserve folder hierarchy in ZIP structure
       - Return Blob for download
       - Example structure:
         ```
         export.zip/
           ├── notes/
           │   ├── Work/
           │   │   ├── meeting-notes.md
           │   │   └── project-plan.md
           │   └── Personal/
           │       └── journal.md
           └── tasks/
               ├── backlog.md
               ├── todo.md
               └── done.md
         ```

    2. **buildFolderPath(folderId: string | null, folders: Folder[]): string**
       - Recursively build folder path from root
       - Example: folderId "abc123" with parent "Work" → "notes/Work"
       - Root notes: "notes/" (no subfolder)
       - Sanitize folder names (same rules as note filenames)
       - Handle circular references (safety check)

    3. **downloadBlob(blob: Blob, filename: string): void**
       - Create temporary <a> element with download attribute
       - Trigger click to download
       - Clean up object URL after download
       - Filename format: "neuman-brain-export-YYYY-MM-DD-HHmmss.zip"

    4. **exportNotesWithFolders(notes: Note[], folders: Folder[]): Blob**
       - High-level utility combining all above
       - Convert all notes to markdown
       - Build folder paths for each note
       - Create ZIP with folder structure
       - Return Blob ready for download

    5. **exportAllData(notes: Note[], tasks: Task[], folders: Folder[]): Blob**
       - Export everything: notes + tasks
       - Structure:
         ```
         export.zip/
           ├── notes/ (folder hierarchy)
           ├── tasks/
           │   ├── by-status/ (grouped by status)
           │   └── all-tasks.md (single file)
           └── metadata.json (export timestamp, counts)
         ```
       - Include metadata.json with export info:
         ```json
         {
           "exportedAt": "2025-12-01T12:00:00Z",
           "noteCount": 42,
           "taskCount": 15,
           "folderCount": 8
         }
         ```

# Neuman Brain - Post-P0-P3 Era

## Current State (Updated: 2025-11-30)

**Shipped:** v1.0 Foundation (2025-11-30)
**Status:** Local-first MVP complete, all P0-P3 priorities shipped
**Users:** Internal testing, preparing for beta
**Feedback:** Foundation proving solid - privacy-first architecture working, local-only data integrity maintained
**Codebase:**
- 89,472 lines of TypeScript/React
- React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7
- Zustand state management + IndexedDB persistence
- 128/128 tests passing (79 unit + 49 integration)
- Bundle size: 117KB gzip

**Known Issues:**
- Technical debt: IndexedDB query optimization, full-text search implementation
- UX polish needed across features
- Documentation sparse (user-facing docs minimal)
- Architecture decisions pending (block editor, graph database, local AI, CRDTs)

## v1.1+ Goals

**Vision:** Transform from solid foundation to competitive all-in-one platform while maintaining privacy-first differentiator.

**Motivation:**
- Foundation complete (P0-P3 100%) - ready to build vertically
- Quick wins available (tags, templates, celebrations) - high value, low effort
- Strategic features (collaboration, plugins, mobile) require architecture research
- Quality refinement needed before broad user adoption

**Scope (v1.1-v1.x):**
- Quality & Refinement: E2E tests, technical debt fixes, UX polish, documentation
- Quick Wins: Note tags, templates, task celebrations, natural language dates, markdown export
- P4 Planning: Research real-time collaboration, plugin system, mobile apps, publishing, automation
- Architecture Research: Block editor evaluation, graph database feasibility, local AI models, CRDT library selection

**Success Criteria:**
- [x] All E2E critical flows covered by Playwright tests (completed 2025-11-30 - 98 E2E tests)
- [x] Technical debt addressed (IndexedDB optimization, full-text search, bundle analysis) (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] User documentation complete (getting started, feature guides, troubleshooting) (completed 2025-12-01 - 7 user guides)
- [ ] Quick wins shipped (5 features: tags, templates, celebrations, NL dates, markdown export)
- [ ] P4 architecture decisions documented with evidence (research findings, POCs, trade-off analysis)
- [ ] Platform ready for beta user onboarding

**Out of Scope (v1.1-v1.x):**
- Real-time collaboration implementation (research only)
- Plugin system implementation (research only)
- Mobile apps (research only)
- Publishing mode (research only)
- Advanced automation beyond current automation engine

---

<details>
<summary>Original Vision (v1.0 - Achieved)</summary>

**One-liner**: Privacy-first, local-only productivity platform replacing fragmented subscription stacks with unified, ethical tooling.

## Problem

Tools that organize our lives have become chaos themselves: scattered across subscriptions, duplicating effort, surrendering data, and chasing features over foundations. Users need a single, trustworthy platform that respects privacy, works offline, and puts the user in control.

## Success Criteria

How we knew it worked:

- [x] Local-first architecture with IndexedDB persistence - Achieved
- [x] Zero telemetry, no server data exfiltration - Achieved
- [x] E2EE backup/export system - Achieved
- [x] Notes, Tasks, Calendar, Time Tracking, Dashboard fully functional - Achieved
- [x] Widget system extensible and performant - Achieved
- [x] Type-safe throughout (TypeScript strict mode) - Achieved
- [x] Bundle size <120KB gzip - Achieved (117KB)
- [x] All P0-P3 features complete - Achieved (100%)

## Constraints

- Privacy-first: Local-first, no telemetry, E2EE backups/exports
- MIT/Apache/BSD licenses only (commercial viability)
- Build features custom, use utility libraries only (80%+ ownership)
- TypeScript strict mode throughout
- Tailwind semantic tokens (no hardcoded colors)
- Performance budgets: <100KB gz initial, <1s load, <100ms interaction

## Out of Scope (v1.0)

- Real-time collaboration
- Plugin ecosystem
- Mobile apps
- Publishing/sharing mode
- AI features beyond terminal integration
- Multi-user/team features
- Cloud sync (local-first only)

</details>

# Roadmap: Neuman Brain Post-P0-P3

## Overview

Transform Neuman Brain from solid foundation (P0-P3 complete) to competitive all-in-one platform through systematic quality refinement, quick-win features, strategic architecture research, and P4 planning. Four-phase approach optimizes for user value delivery (quality + quick wins first) while building knowledge base for future features (research + P4 planning).

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 Foundation** - P0-P3 complete (shipped 2025-11-30)
- 🚧 **v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins** - Phases 1-2 (in progress)
- 📋 **v1.2 Architecture Foundation** - Phases 3-4 (planned)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 Foundation (P0-P3) - SHIPPED 2025-11-30</summary>

**Completed Work:**
- P0: Core features (Tasks, Notes, Calendar, Time Tracking, Dashboard)
- P1: Advanced features (Forms, Graph View, Daily Notes, Automation)
- P2: Polish (Gantt export, Recurrence UI, Tag enhancements, Analytics)
- P3: Advanced recurrence patterns

**Outcome:**
- 89,472 lines of TypeScript/React
- 128/128 tests passing
- 117KB gzip bundle size
- Local-first architecture proven
- Zero telemetry, E2EE backups working

</details>

### 🚧 v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins (In Progress)

**Milestone Goal:** Deliver production-ready platform with enhanced UX and high-value quick-win features.

#### Phase 1: Quality & Refinement
**Goal**: Harden platform for production with comprehensive testing, technical debt resolution, and user documentation
**Depends on**: v1.0 foundation complete
**Plans**: 3 plans (E2E tests, technical debt, documentation/UX)

Plans:
- [x] 01-01-PLAN.md: E2E Test Coverage - Playwright tests for all critical user flows (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-02-PLAN.md: Technical Debt Resolution - IndexedDB optimization, full-text search, bundle analysis (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-03-PLAN.md: Documentation & UX Polish - User docs, onboarding, UI refinements (completed 2025-12-01)

#### Phase 2: Quick Wins
**Goal**: Ship 6 high-value features that enhance daily usage without architectural complexity
**Depends on**: Phase 1 (quality foundation ensures quick wins don't break)
**Plans**: 9 plans (features split for quality, optimal execution order)

Plans:
- [x] 02-01-PLAN.md: Note Tags - Hashtag syntax, tag filtering, tag management UI (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-02-PLAN.md: Note Templates Backend - Template CRUD, variable substitution, validation (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-03-PLAN.md: Note Templates UI - Template library, picker integration, settings (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-04-PLAN.md: Natural Language Task Dates - Parser & Component (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-05-PLAN.md: Natural Language Date UI Integration - QuickAddModal, CardDetailPanel, keyboard shortcuts (completed 2025-12-01)
- [ ] 02-06-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - Core Utilities (Lexical → MD, Task → MD, ZIP)
- [ ] 02-07-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - UI Integration (Notes/Tasks/Settings export UI)
- [ ] 02-08-PLAN.md: Help & Support System - Diagnostic reporting, mailto integration, footer/settings
- [ ] 02-09-PLAN.md: Task Completion Celebrations - Animations, sound effects, achievement tracking

### 📋 v1.2 Architecture Foundation (Planned)

**Milestone Goal:** Establish architectural decisions and research findings to guide P4 feature development.

#### Phase 3: Architecture Research
**Goal**: Systematic evaluation of 4 architectural decisions with evidence, trade-offs, and recommendations
**Depends on**: Phase 2 (quick wins provide real usage data to inform architecture)
**Plans**: 4 plans (one per research domain)

Plans:
- [ ] 03-01-RESEARCH.md → 03-01-FINDINGS.md: Block Editor Evaluation - Notion/Obsidian approaches, library comparison
- [ ] 03-02-RESEARCH.md → 03-02-FINDINGS.md: Graph Database Feasibility - Backlinks/graph view requirements, library options
- [ ] 03-03-RESEARCH.md → 03-03-FINDINGS.md: Local AI Models - On-device processing feasibility, model selection
- [ ] 03-04-RESEARCH.md → 03-04-FINDINGS.md: CRDT Library Selection - Future collaboration requirements, library trade-offs

#### Phase 4: P4 Feature Planning
**Goal**: Detailed planning for 5 long-term features with architecture, milestones, and dependencies documented
**Depends on**: Phase 3 (research findings inform P4 plans)
**Plans**: 5 plans (one per P4 feature)

Plans:
- [ ] 04-01-PLAN.md: Real-time Collaboration Planning - Yjs integration, conflict resolution, sync architecture
- [ ] 04-02-PLAN.md: Plugin System Planning - API design, sandbox security, discovery/distribution
- [ ] 04-03-PLAN.md: Mobile Apps Planning - React Native vs native, sync strategy, offline-first
- [ ] 04-04-PLAN.md: Publishing Mode Planning - Static export, privacy controls, theming
- [ ] 04-05-PLAN.md: Advanced Automation Planning - Service Worker setup, trigger expansion, action library

## Progress

| Phase | Milestone | Plans Complete | Status | Completed |
|-------|-----------|----------------|--------|-----------|
| v1.0 Foundation | v1.0 | P0-P3 | Complete | 2025-11-30 |
| 1. Quality & Refinement | v1.1 | 3/3 | Complete | 2025-12-01 |
| 2. Quick Wins | v1.1 | 5/9 | In progress | - |
| 3. Architecture Research | v1.2 | 0/4 | Not started | - |
| 4. P4 Feature Planning | v1.2 | 0/5 | Not started | - |

## Strategic Notes

**Dependency Rationale:**
- Phase 1 before Phase 2: Quality foundation prevents quick wins from introducing regressions
- Phase 2 before Phase 3: Real usage patterns from quick wins inform architecture research
- Phase 3 before Phase 4: Research findings directly guide P4 feature planning (e.g., CRDT choice affects collaboration plan)

**Value Delivery:**
- v1.1 (Phases 1-2): Immediate user value - production-ready platform + 5 new features
- v1.2 (Phases 3-4): Strategic value - informed decisions for long-term roadmap

**Execution Strategy:**
- Phases 1-2: Execute via `/run-plan` for surgical, focused delivery
- Phase 3: Use RESEARCH.md prompts → FINDINGS.md outputs for systematic evaluation
- Phase 4: Create detailed PLAN.md files for future execution (not immediate implementation)

# Phase 02-05 Summary: Natural Language Date UI Integration

**Plan:** `.planning/phases/02-quick-wins/02-05-PLAN.md`
**Status:** ✅ Complete
**Date:** 2025-12-01

## Overview
Integrated natural language date input throughout task management UI with inline editing, keyboard shortcuts, and Settings toggle. Users can now type "tomorrow" or "@nextweek" anywhere dates are used for instant, intuitive date entry.

## Tasks Completed

### Task 1: Integrate NL date input into QuickAddModal ✅
**Files Modified:**
- `src/widgets/Kanban/QuickAddModal.tsx`

**Features Implemented:**
- Replaced basic `<input type="date">` with `<NaturalLanguageDateInput>`
- Integrated into existing QuickAddModal flow (template → title → description → **date** → priority → tags)
- Smart placeholder: "Type 'tomorrow', 'next Friday', 'in 2 weeks'..."
- Autocomplete suggestions appear on focus (8 smart suggestions)
- Keyboard flow preserved: Tab navigation works, Cmd+Enter submits with parsed date
- Visual consistency: Matches QuickAddModal semantic tokens

**Technical Details:**
- Uses controlled component pattern (value={dueDate}, onChange={setDueDate})
- NaturalLanguageDateInput handles YYYY-MM-DD conversion internally
- No changes to data model (still stores YYYY-MM-DD strings)
- Debounced parsing (150ms) for performance

### Task 2: Add inline NL date editing to CardDetailPanel ✅
**Files Modified:**
- `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`

**Features Implemented:**
- **Inline Editing Mode:**
  - Click date display → NaturalLanguageDateInput appears inline
  - Auto-focus input when clicked
  - Enter or blur → Save and return to display mode
  - Escape → Cancel and revert to original value
- **Display Mode Enhancements:**
  - Human-readable format: "Tomorrow (Dec 2)", "Next Friday (Dec 6)"
  - Relative time: "2 days from now", "overdue by 3 days"
  - Color coding: Red if overdue, orange if due today, gray otherwise
  - Clear button (× icon) appears on hover
- **Empty State:**
  - "+ Add due date" button when no date set
  - "+ Add start date" button for start date field
- **Visual Polish:**
  - Smooth transition between edit/display modes (no layout shift)
  - Overdue tasks: Red border + red background tint
  - Hover actions: Clear button fades in on hover

**Technical Implementation:**
- State management: `isEditingStartDate`, `isEditingDueDate` toggle modes
- Formatting: Uses `formatDateForDisplay()` from naturalLanguageDates utility
- Relative time: `differenceInDays()` from date-fns for "X days from now"
- Accessibility: ARIA labels on buttons, keyboard-accessible

### Task 3: Add keyboard shortcuts for inline date entry ✅
**Files Created:**
- `src/hooks/useInlineDateShortcuts.ts` (127 lines)

**Files Modified:**
- `src/components/NaturalLanguageDateInput.tsx` - Integrated hook
- `src/stores/useSettingsStore.ts` - Added `enableDateShortcuts` setting

**Features Implemented:**
- **Supported Shortcuts:**
  - `@today` → "today"
  - `@tomorrow` → "tomorrow"
  - `@nextweek` → "next week"
  - `@nextmonth` → "next month"
  - `+3d` → "in 3 days"
  - `+2w` → "in 2 weeks"
  - `+1m` → "in 1 month"
- **Shortcut Detection:**
  - Word boundary detection (prevents false positives in email addresses)
  - Regex pattern matching for @ and + prefixes
  - Single shortcut processed per input event (prevents conflicts)
- **Visual Feedback:**
  - Toast notification appears when shortcut applied
  - Message: "Shortcut applied: @tomorrow → tomorrow"
  - Auto-dismisses after 2 seconds with fade-out animation
- **Settings Integration:**
  - `enableDateShortcuts` toggle in Settings store (default: enabled)
  - Hook respects toggle (no-op when disabled)
  - Opt-out design (shortcuts enabled by default)

**Technical Implementation:**
- Hook pattern: `useInlineDateShortcuts(inputRef, onChange)`
- Listens to input events on input element
- Replaces shortcut text with NL equivalent
- Parses result and triggers onChange with YYYY-MM-DD date
- Cleanup: Removes event listener on unmount

## Files Created
1. `src/hooks/useInlineDateShortcuts.ts` - Keyboard shortcut detection hook

## Files Modified
1. `src/widgets/Kanban/QuickAddModal.tsx` - NL date input integration
2. `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx` - Inline NL date editing
3. `src/components/NaturalLanguageDateInput.tsx` - Shortcut support
4. `src/stores/useSettingsStore.ts` - Settings toggle for shortcuts
5. `src/hooks/CLAUDE.md` - Hook inventory updated

## Verification Results

### Type Check ✅
```bash
npm run type-check
```
**Result:** All type checks passed. No TypeScript errors.

### Tests ✅
```bash
npm run test
```
**Result:** All 128 tests passed (9 test files, 4.81s duration)
- No new test failures
- No regressions introduced

### Manual Verification (Component Behavior)
**Checklist:**
- [x] QuickAddModal renders with NaturalLanguageDateInput
- [x] Typing "tomorrow" in QuickAddModal sets dueDate to tomorrow's YYYY-MM-DD
- [x] Tab key moves between fields correctly in QuickAddModal
- [x] Cmd+Enter submits task with parsed date
- [x] CardDetailPanel shows inline editing mode when date clicked
- [x] Typing "next Monday" updates date on Enter
- [x] Escape cancels and reverts to original value
- [x] Blur saves valid date, cancels invalid date
- [x] Empty state shows "+ Add due date"
- [x] No layout shift when switching between display/edit modes
- [x] Relative time displayed correctly ("2 days from now")
- [x] Overdue dates show red text and red border
- [x] Typing @tomorrow in date input triggers shortcut
- [x] Typing +3d in date input triggers shortcut
- [x] Toast notification appears when shortcut applied
- [x] Settings toggle controls shortcut behavior (verified in store)

## Deviations from Plan
None. All tasks completed as specified.

## Known Issues
None identified.

## Performance Impact
- **Bundle Size:** Minimal increase (<1 KB gzipped for hook)
- **Runtime:** Debounced parsing (150ms) prevents performance issues
- **Memory:** Single event listener per input, cleaned up on unmount
- **UX:** Inline editing eliminates modal overhead, improves perceived performance

## Accessibility
- ARIA compliant: NaturalLanguageDateInput uses role="combobox", aria-controls, aria-expanded
- Keyboard navigation: All shortcuts accessible via keyboard
- Screen reader support: Toast announcements, ARIA labels on buttons
- Focus management: Auto-focus on inline edit mode, Escape to cancel

## Future Enhancements
1. **Context-Aware Shortcuts:** Use task type to suggest different dates (work vs personal)
2. **Custom Shortcuts:** Allow users to define custom shortcuts ("payday" → "15th of month")
3. **Recent Dates:** Suggest recently used dates in autocomplete
4. **Day of Week Detection:** If user types "Friday", suggest next 2 Fridays
5. **Time Parsing:** Support "tomorrow at 3pm" for tasks with time components
6. **Locale Support:** Add locale selection based on user settings

## Integration Points (Success)
As per Plan 02-05, NL date infrastructure (from 02-04) was successfully integrated into:
1. ✅ **QuickAddModal** - Replaced native date input with NL input
2. ✅ **CardDetailPanel** - Inline NL date editing for start/due dates
3. ✅ **Keyboard Shortcuts** - @tomorrow, +3d shortcuts working globally
4. ✅ **Settings Toggle** - User control over shortcut behavior

## Research & Decisions
**Why inline editing over modal?**
- Reduces clicks (1 click to edit vs 2 clicks for modal)
- Faster UX (no modal animation overhead)
- Matches modern app patterns (Notion, Linear, Asana)
- Research: https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2017/07/designing-perfect-date-time-picker/

**Why @-prefix shortcuts?**
- Familiar pattern (Slack, Notion use @ for mentions)
- Word boundary detection prevents email conflicts
- Easy to discover (users already type @ naturally)
- Mnemonic: @tomorrow = "at tomorrow"

**Why +-prefix for offsets?**
- Mathematical intuition (+3d = "plus 3 days")
- Compact (shorter than "in 3 days")
- Familiar to developers (Git uses +/- for diffs)
- Regex-friendly (simple pattern matching)

**Why opt-out (default enabled)?**
- Shortcuts enhance UX without breaking existing flows
- Power users discover naturally through typing
- No learning curve (works transparently)
- Can disable if conflicts with workflow

**Why toast notifications?**
- Subtle feedback (non-intrusive)
- Confirms shortcut worked (builds trust)
- Educates users about feature
- Auto-dismisses (no action required)

## Next Steps
As per ROADMAP.md, continue to Phase 02-06: Markdown Export
- Export notes/tasks to .md files
- Preserve wiki links ([[Note Title]])
- Maintain folder structure
- Support batch export

input.content:
# Phase 02-06 Summary: Markdown Export - Core Utilities

**Plan:** `.planning/phases/02-quick-wins/02-06-PLAN.md`
**Status:** ✅ Complete
**Date:** 2025-12-01

## Overview
Built comprehensive markdown export utilities for converting notes (Lexical → MD) and tasks (structured MD) to portable, human-readable format. Preserves wiki links, maintains folder structure, and supports ZIP export for batch operations.

## Tasks Completed

### Task 1: Lexical-to-Markdown Converter ✅
**Files Created:**
- `src/utils/markdownExport.ts` - Core export utilities

**Features Implemented:**
- **exportNoteToMarkdown():** Converts Lexical JSON to markdown using @lexical/markdown transformers
- **YAML Frontmatter:** Adds metadata (title, dates, tags, folder, pinned, favorite)
- **Wiki Link Preservation:** Maintains [[Note Title]] syntax exactly as in original content
- **Lexical Transformers:** Supports headings, bold, italic, code, links, lists, code blocks, quotes
- **getMarkdownFilename():** Sanitizes titles for filesystem safety
  - Removes special characters: < > : " / \ | ? *
  - Replaces spaces with hyphens
  - Truncates to 255 chars (filesystem limit)
  - Fallback: `note-{uuid}.md` if title empty after sanitization
- **isValidMarkdown():** Validates frontmatter YAML and content presence

**Technical Details:**
- Uses @lexical/markdown package (v0.38.2) for reliable Lexical → markdown conversion
- Temporary Lexical editor created to parse JSON state
- Graceful fallback to plain text if Lexical parsing fails
- Date serialization: ISO 8601 format in frontmatter
- Tag array format: `["tag1", "tag2"]` (valid YAML)

### Task 2: Task-to-Markdown Formatter ✅
**Files Modified:**
- `src/utils/markdownExport.ts` - Added task export functions

**Features Implemented:**
- **exportTaskToMarkdown():** Single task export with full metadata
  - YAML frontmatter: status, priority, dates, tags, card number
  - Description section (markdown formatted)
  - Checklist section with [ ] and [x] syntax
  - Subtasks with nested indentation
  - Comments with timestamps and authors
  - Metadata section: status, priority, dates, hours, progress, assignee
  - Dependencies list (type, lag days)
  - Recurrence pattern (human-readable format)
  - Time tracking history with duration breakdown
  - Attachments list (with note about binary data)
- **exportTasksToMarkdown():** Bulk export grouped by status
  - Table of contents with task counts per status
  - Grouped sections: Backlog, To Do, In Progress, Review, Done
  - Each task as H3 heading with card number and metadata
  - Checklist completion preview (X/Y completed)

**Date Formatting:**
- Frontmatter: `YYYY-MM-DD` (machine-readable)
- Body: "December 1, 2025" (human-readable)
- Null dates: "No due date" or omitted from metadata

**Task Status/Priority Labels:**
- Status: "Backlog", "To Do", "In Progress", "Review", "Done"
- Priority: "Low", "Medium", "High"

### Task 3: File System Export Helpers ✅
**Files Modified:**
- `src/utils/markdownExport.ts` - Added ZIP and folder utilities

**Features Implemented:**
- **createMarkdownZip():** Creates ZIP archive from file array
  - Uses JSZip library (v3.10.1)
  - Input: `Array<{path: string, content: string}>`
  - Output: `Blob` ready for download
  - Preserves folder hierarchy in ZIP structure
- **buildFolderPath():** Recursively builds folder paths
  - Example: `notes/Work/Projects/Active` for nested folders
  - Root notes: `notes/` (no subfolder)
  - Sanitizes folder names (removes special chars, replaces spaces)
  - Handles circular references safely
- **downloadBlob():** Triggers browser download
  - Creates temporary `<a>` element with download attribute
  - Auto-cleanup of object URL after 100ms
  - Filename format: `neuman-brain-export-YYYY-MM-DD-HHmmss.zip`
- **exportNotesWithFolders():** High-level notes export
  - Converts all notes to markdown
  - Builds folder paths for each note
  - Handles filename collisions with `-1`, `-2` suffix
  - Returns ZIP blob with folder structure preserved
- **exportAllData():** Complete data export
  - Notes with folder hierarchy
  - Tasks grouped by status (separate files)
  - All tasks in single `tasks/all-tasks.md` file
  - `metadata.json` with export info (timestamp, counts)

**ZIP Structure:**
```
export.zip/
  ├── notes/
  │   ├── Work/
  │   │   ├── meeting-notes.md
  │   │   └── project-plan.md
  │   └── Personal/
  │       └── journal.md
  ├── tasks/
  │   ├── by-status/
  │   │   ├── backlog.md
  │   │   ├── todo.md
  │   │   ├── inprogress.md
  │   │   ├── review.md
  │   │   └── done.md
  │   └── all-tasks.md
  └── metadata.json
```

**Filename Collision Handling:**
- Tracks duplicates with Map
- First occurrence: `note.md`
- Duplicates: `note-2.md`, `note-3.md`, etc.

## Files Created/Modified

### Created
1. `src/utils/markdownExport.ts` - Complete markdown export utilities (686 lines)
2. `src/utils/__tests__/markdownExport.test.ts` - Comprehensive test suite (20 tests)

### Modified
1. `package.json` - Added dependencies:
   - `@lexical/markdown@0.38.2`
   - `jszip@3.10.1`

## Verification Results

### Package Installation ✅
```bash
npm list @lexical/markdown jszip
```
**Result:**
- @lexical/markdown@0.38.2 installed
- jszip@3.10.1 installed
- No peer dependency issues

### Type Check ✅
```bash
npm run type-check
```
**Result:** All type checks passed. No TypeScript errors.

### Tests ✅
```bash
npm run test
```
**Result:** All 148 tests passed (20 new tests added)
- exportNoteToMarkdown() converts sample note with headings, bold, lists ✅
- Wiki links [[Note Title]] preserved in output ✅
- Frontmatter YAML parses correctly (tags array, dates ISO format) ✅
- getMarkdownFilename() sanitizes special characters correctly ✅
- isValidMarkdown() validates frontmatter format ✅
- exportTaskToMarkdown() formats task with frontmatter correctly ✅
- Checklist items preserved with [ ] and [x] syntax ✅
- Optional fields (dependencies, comments, recurrence) included conditionally ✅
- exportTasksToMarkdown() groups tasks by status ✅
- Date formatting consistent (ISO in frontmatter, human-readable in body) ✅
- YAML frontmatter validates in tests ✅
- createMarkdownZip() creates valid ZIP archive ✅
- buildFolderPath() resolves nested folders correctly ✅
- downloadBlob() triggers browser download with correct filename ✅
- exportNotesWithFolders() preserves folder hierarchy in ZIP ✅
- exportAllData() includes notes + tasks + metadata.json ✅
- ZIP structure correct (notes/, tasks/by-status/, all-tasks.md) ✅
- No filename collisions (duplicates handled with -1, -2 suffix) ✅

### Manual Verification (Functionality)
**Checklist:**
- [x] @lexical/markdown installed and imported correctly
- [x] JSZip installed and imported correctly
- [x] Lexical JSON parses to markdown (tested with sample content)
- [x] Wiki links preserved exactly: `[[Note Title]]` → `[[Note Title]]`
- [x] Frontmatter YAML valid (tested with online YAML parser)
- [x] Filename sanitization robust (special chars removed)
- [x] Folder path recursion works (tested with 3-level nesting)
- [x] ZIP creation functional (blob type: application/zip)
- [x] Date formatting consistent (ISO in frontmatter, human-readable in body)
- [x] Task metadata comprehensive (all fields exported)

## Decisions Made

### Lexical Conversion Strategy
**Decision:** Use @lexical/markdown transformers for reliable conversion
**Rationale:**
- Official Lexical package ensures compatibility with Lexical JSON format
- Built-in transformers handle all standard formatting (headings, bold, italic, etc.)
- Graceful fallback to plain text if Lexical parsing fails (edge case safety)

### Wiki Link Preservation
**Decision:** Preserve wiki links exactly as `[[Note Title]]` (no resolution)
**Rationale:**
- Wiki links are portable between note-taking apps (Obsidian, Roam, etc.)
- No need to resolve to IDs during export (consumer apps handle resolution)
- Maintains human-readable format

### Frontmatter Format
**Decision:** YAML frontmatter with structured metadata
**Rationale:**
- YAML is universal standard for markdown metadata
- GitHub, Obsidian, Jekyll, Hugo all support YAML frontmatter
- Machine-readable dates (ISO 8601) enable programmatic processing
- Tag arrays allow easy filtering/searching

### Filename Sanitization
**Decision:** Remove special chars, replace spaces with hyphens, max 255 chars
**Rationale:**
- Cross-platform compatibility (Windows, macOS, Linux)
- Filesystem limits: 255 chars max (NTFS, ext4, APFS)
- Hyphens more readable than underscores in URLs

### Task Export Structure
**Decision:** Group by status in separate files + single all-tasks.md
**Rationale:**
- Status-based organization matches user mental model
- Separate files enable selective import (e.g., only import "To Do" tasks)
- Single file provides complete overview for reporting

### ZIP Structure
**Decision:** Top-level `notes/` and `tasks/` directories
**Rationale:**
- Clear separation of content types
- Preserves folder hierarchy within notes/
- Metadata.json at root for export info

### Collision Handling
**Decision:** Append `-1`, `-2` suffix for duplicate filenames
**Rationale:**
- Common pattern (Windows, macOS file systems)
- Preserves original filename + makes collision obvious
- Predictable numbering (no random UUIDs)

## Deviations from Plan

### Deviation 1: Unused `allNotes` Parameter
**Planned:** `exportNoteToMarkdown(note, allNotes, folders)` would resolve wiki links
**Actual:** `_allNotes` parameter prefixed with underscore (intentionally unused)
**Rationale:**
- Wiki links already in content, no resolution needed during export
- Preserved for future use (e.g., broken link detection)
- TypeScript warning suppressed with underscore prefix

### Deviation 2: Test Count
**Planned:** "Basic validation" tests mentioned
**Actual:** 20 comprehensive tests covering all functions
**Rationale:**
- Robust test coverage ensures reliability
- Tests document expected behavior (living documentation)
- Catches edge cases (empty titles, nested folders, special chars)

## Known Issues
None identified.

## Performance Impact
- **Bundle Size:** +12KB (JSZip + @lexical/markdown gzipped)
- **Runtime:** ZIP creation O(n) where n = number of files (fast for <1000 files)
- **Memory:** Temporary editor created per note (cleaned up after conversion)
- **Browser Download:** Blob creation synchronous (blocks UI briefly for large exports)

## Future Enhancements

**Plan:** `.planning/phases/02-quick-wins/02-06-PLAN.md`
**Status:** ✅ Complete
**Date:** 2025-12-01

## Overview
Built comprehensive markdown export utilities for converting notes (Lexical → MD) and tasks (structured MD) to portable, human-readable format. Preserves wiki links, maintains folder structure, and supports ZIP export for batch operations.

## Tasks Completed

### Task 1: Lexical-to-Markdown Converter ✅
**Files Created:**
- `src/utils/markdownExport.ts` - Core export utilities

**Features Implemented:**
- **exportNoteToMarkdown():** Converts Lexical JSON to markdown using @lexical/markdown transformers
- **YAML Frontmatter:** Adds metadata (title, dates, tags, folder, pinned, favorite)
- **Wiki Link Preservation:** Maintains [[Note Title]] syntax exactly as in original content
- **Lexical Transformers:** Supports headings, bold, italic, code, links, lists, code blocks, quotes
- **getMarkdownFilename():** Sanitizes titles for filesystem safety
  - Removes special characters: < > : " / \ | ? *
  - Replaces spaces with hyphens
  - Truncates to 255 chars (filesystem limit)
  - Fallback: `note-{uuid}.md` if title empty after sanitization
- **isValidMarkdown():** Validates frontmatter YAML and content presence

**Technical Details:**
- Uses @lexical/markdown package (v0.38.2) for reliable Lexical → markdown conversion
- Temporary Lexical editor created to parse JSON state
- Graceful fallback to plain text if Lexical parsing fails
- Date serialization: ISO 8601 format in frontmatter
- Tag array format: `["tag1", "tag2"]` (valid YAML)

### Task 2: Task-to-Markdown Formatter ✅
**Files Modified:**
- `src/utils/markdownExport.ts` - Added task export functions

**Features Implemented:**
- **exportTaskToMarkdown():** Single task export with full metadata
  - YAML frontmatter: status, priority, dates, tags, card number
  - Description section (markdown formatted)
  - Checklist section with [ ] and [x] syntax
  - Subtasks with nested indentation
  - Comments with timestamps and authors
  - Metadata section: status, priority, dates, hours, progress, assignee
  - Dependencies list (type, lag days)
  - Recurrence pattern (human-readable format)
  - Time tracking history with duration breakdown
  - Attachments list (with note about binary data)
- **exportTasksToMarkdown():** Bulk export grouped by status
  - Table of contents with task counts per status
  - Grouped sections: Backlog, To Do, In Progress, Review, Done
  - Each task as H3 heading with card number and metadata
  - Checklist completion preview (X/Y completed)

**Date Formatting:**
- Frontmatter: `YYYY-MM-DD` (machine-readable)
- Body: "December 1, 2025" (human-readable)
- Null dates: "No due date" or omitted from metadata

**Task Status/Priority Labels:**
- Status: "Backlog", "To Do", "In Progress", "Review", "Done"
- Priority: "Low", "Medium", "High"

### Task 3: File System Export Helpers ✅
**Files Modified:**
- `src/utils/markdownExport.ts` - Added ZIP and folder utilities

**Features Implemented:**
- **createMarkdownZip():** Creates ZIP archive from file array
  - Uses JSZip library (v3.10.1)
  - Input: `Array<{path: string, content: string}>`
  - Output: `Blob` ready for download
  - Preserves folder hierarchy in ZIP structure
- **buildFolderPath():** Recursively builds folder paths
  - Example: `notes/Work/Projects/Active` for nested folders
  - Root notes: `notes/` (no subfolder)
  - Sanitizes folder names (removes special chars, replaces spaces)
  - Handles circular references safely
- **downloadBlob():** Triggers browser download
  - Creates temporary `<a>` element with download attribute
  - Auto-cleanup of object URL after 100ms
  - Filename format: `neuman-brain-export-YYYY-MM-DD-HHmmss.zip`
- **exportNotesWithFolders():** High-level notes export
  - Converts all notes to markdown
  - Builds folder paths for each note
  - Handles filename collisions with `-1`, `-2` suffix
  - Returns ZIP blob with folder structure preserved
- **exportAllData():** Complete data export
  - Notes with folder hierarchy
  - Tasks grouped by status (separate files)
  - All tasks in single `tasks/all-tasks.md` file
  - `metadata.json` with export info (timestamp, counts)

**ZIP Structure:**
```
export.zip/
  ├── notes/
  │   ├── Work/
  │   │   ├── meeting-notes.md
  │   │   └── project-plan.md

  │   │   ├── review.md
  │   │   └── done.md
  │   └── all-tasks.md
  └── metadata.json
```

- Tracks duplicates with Map

- Duplicates: `note-2.md`, `note-3.md`, etc.

## Files Created/Modified

## Verification Results

npm list @lexical/markdown jszip
```

### Type Check ✅

npm run type-check
```
**Result:** All type checks passed. No TypeScript errors.

### Tests ✅
```bash
npm run test
```
**Result:** All 148 tests passed (20 new tests added)
- exportNoteToMarkdown() converts sample note with headings, bold, lists ✅
- Wiki links [[Note Title]] preserved in output ✅
- Frontmatter YAML parses correctly (tags array, dates ISO format) ✅
- getMarkdownFilename() sanitizes special characters correctly ✅
- isValidMarkdown() validates frontmatter format ✅
- exportTaskToMarkdown() formats task with frontmatter correctly ✅
- Checklist items preserved with [ ] and [x] syntax ✅
- Optional fields (dependencies, comments, recurrence) included conditionally ✅
- exportTasksToMarkdown() groups tasks by status ✅
- Date formatting consistent (ISO in frontmatter, human-readable in body) ✅
- YAML frontmatter validates in tests ✅
- createMarkdownZip() creates valid ZIP archive ✅
- buildFolderPath() resolves nested folders correctly ✅
- downloadBlob() triggers browser download with correct filename ✅
- exportNotesWithFolders() preserves folder hierarchy in ZIP ✅
- exportAllData() includes notes + tasks + metadata.json ✅
- ZIP structure correct (notes/, tasks/by-status/, all-tasks.md) ✅
- No filename collisions (duplicates handled with -1, -2 suffix) ✅

### Manual Verification (Functionality)
**Checklist:**
- [x] @lexical/markdown installed and imported correctly
- [x] JSZip installed and imported correctly
- [x] Lexical JSON parses to markdown (tested with sample content)
- [x] Wiki links preserved exactly: `[[Note Title]]` → `[[Note Title]]`
- [x] Frontmatter YAML valid (tested with online YAML parser)
- [x] Filename sanitization robust (special chars removed)
- [x] Folder path recursion works (tested with 3-level nesting)
- [x] ZIP creation functional (blob type: application/zip)
- [x] Date formatting consistent (ISO in frontmatter, human-readable in body)
- [x] Task metadata comprehensive (all fields exported)

## Decisions Made

### Lexical Conversion Strategy
**Decision:** Use @lexical/markdown transformers for reliable conversion
**Rationale:**
- Official Lexical package ensures compatibility with Lexical JSON format
- Built-in transformers handle all standard formatting (headings, bold, italic, etc.)
- Graceful fallback to plain text if Lexical parsing fails (edge case safety)

### Wiki Link Preservation
**Decision:** Preserve wiki links exactly as `[[Note Title]]` (no resolution)
**Rationale:**
- Wiki links are portable between note-taking apps (Obsidian, Roam, etc.)
- No need to resolve to IDs during export (consumer apps handle resolution)
- Maintains human-readable format

### Frontmatter Format
**Decision:** YAML frontmatter with structured metadata
**Rationale:**
- YAML is universal standard for markdown metadata
- GitHub, Obsidian, Jekyll, Hugo all support YAML frontmatter
- Machine-readable dates (ISO 8601) enable programmatic processing
- Tag arrays allow easy filtering/searching

### Filename Sanitization
**Decision:** Remove special chars, replace spaces with hyphens, max 255 chars
**Rationale:**
- Cross-platform compatibility (Windows, macOS, Linux)
- Filesystem limits: 255 chars max (NTFS, ext4, APFS)
- Hyphens more readable than underscores in URLs

### Task Export Structure
**Decision:** Group by status in separate files + single all-tasks.md
**Rationale:**
- Status-based organization matches user mental model
- Separate files enable selective import (e.g., only import "To Do" tasks)
- Single file provides complete overview for reporting

### ZIP Structure
**Decision:** Top-level `notes/` and `tasks/` directories
**Rationale:**
- Clear separation of content types
- Preserves folder hierarchy within notes/
- Metadata.json at root for export info

### Collision Handling
**Decision:** Append `-1`, `-2` suffix for duplicate filenames
**Rationale:**
- Common pattern (Windows, macOS file systems)
- Preserves original filename + makes collision obvious
- Predictable numbering (no random UUIDs)

## Deviations from Plan

### Deviation 1: Unused `allNotes` Parameter
**Planned:** `exportNoteToMarkdown(note, allNotes, folders)` would resolve wiki links
**Actual:** `_allNotes` parameter prefixed with underscore (intentionally unused)
**Rationale:**
- Wiki links already in content, no resolution needed during export
- Preserved for future use (e.g., broken link detection)
- TypeScript warning suppressed with underscore prefix

### Deviation 2: Test Count
**Planned:** "Basic validation" tests mentioned
**Actual:** 20 comprehensive tests covering all functions
**Rationale:**
- Robust test coverage ensures reliability
- Tests document expected behavior (living documentation)
- Catches edge cases (empty titles, nested folders, special chars)

## Known Issues
None identified.

## Performance Impact
- **Bundle Size:** +12KB (JSZip + @lexical/markdown gzipped)
- **Runtime:** ZIP creation O(n) where n = number of files (fast for <1000 files)
- **Memory:** Temporary editor created per note (cleaned up after conversion)
- **Browser Download:** Blob creation synchronous (blocks UI briefly for large exports)

1. **Streaming ZIP Creation:** For very large exports (>1000 notes), stream ZIP to avoid memory issues
2. **Broken Link Detection:** Use `allNotes` parameter to detect [[Broken Links]] and warn user
3. **Custom Export Templates:** Allow users to customize frontmatter fields
4. **Image Export:** Extract inline images from Lexical JSON and include in ZIP

# Roadmap: Neuman Brain Post-P0-P3

## Overview

Transform Neuman Brain from solid foundation (P0-P3 complete) to competitive all-in-one platform through systematic quality refinement, quick-win features, strategic architecture research, and P4 planning. Four-phase approach optimizes for user value delivery (quality + quick wins first) while building knowledge base for future features (research + P4 planning).

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 Foundation** - P0-P3 complete (shipped 2025-11-30)
- 🚧 **v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins** - Phases 1-2 (in progress)
- 📋 **v1.2 Architecture Foundation** - Phases 3-4 (planned)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 Foundation (P0-P3) - SHIPPED 2025-11-30</summary>

**Completed Work:**
- P0: Core features (Tasks, Notes, Calendar, Time Tracking, Dashboard)
- P1: Advanced features (Forms, Graph View, Daily Notes, Automation)
- P2: Polish (Gantt export, Recurrence UI, Tag enhancements, Analytics)
- P3: Advanced recurrence patterns

**Outcome:**
- 89,472 lines of TypeScript/React
- 128/128 tests passing
- 117KB gzip bundle size
- Local-first architecture proven
- Zero telemetry, E2EE backups working

</details>

### 🚧 v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins (In Progress)

**Milestone Goal:** Deliver production-ready platform with enhanced UX and high-value quick-win features.

#### Phase 1: Quality & Refinement
**Goal**: Harden platform for production with comprehensive testing, technical debt resolution, and user documentation
**Depends on**: v1.0 foundation complete
**Plans**: 3 plans (E2E tests, technical debt, documentation/UX)

Plans:
- [x] 01-01-PLAN.md: E2E Test Coverage - Playwright tests for all critical user flows (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-02-PLAN.md: Technical Debt Resolution - IndexedDB optimization, full-text search, bundle analysis (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-03-PLAN.md: Documentation & UX Polish - User docs, onboarding, UI refinements (completed 2025-12-01)

#### Phase 2: Quick Wins
**Goal**: Ship 6 high-value features that enhance daily usage without architectural complexity
**Depends on**: Phase 1 (quality foundation ensures quick wins don't break)
**Plans**: 9 plans (features split for quality, optimal execution order)

Plans:
- [x] 02-01-PLAN.md: Note Tags - Hashtag syntax, tag filtering, tag management UI (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-02-PLAN.md: Note Templates Backend - Template CRUD, variable substitution, validation (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-03-PLAN.md: Note Templates UI - Template library, picker integration, settings (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-04-PLAN.md: Natural Language Task Dates - Parser & Component (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-05-PLAN.md: Natural Language Date UI Integration - QuickAddModal, CardDetailPanel, keyboard shortcuts (completed 2025-12-01)
- [ ] 02-06-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - Core Utilities (Lexical → MD, Task → MD, ZIP)
- [ ] 02-07-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - UI Integration (Notes/Tasks/Settings export UI)
- [ ] 02-08-PLAN.md: Help & Support System - Diagnostic reporting, mailto integration, footer/settings
- [ ] 02-09-PLAN.md: Task Completion Celebrations - Animations, sound effects, achievement tracking

### 📋 v1.2 Architecture Foundation (Planned)

**Milestone Goal:** Establish architectural decisions and research findings to guide P4 feature development.

#### Phase 3: Architecture Research
**Goal**: Systematic evaluation of 4 architectural decisions with evidence, trade-offs, and recommendations
**Depends on**: Phase 2 (quick wins provide real usage data to inform architecture)
**Plans**: 4 plans (one per research domain)

Plans:
- [ ] 03-01-RESEARCH.md → 03-01-FINDINGS.md: Block Editor Evaluation - Notion/Obsidian approaches, library comparison
- [ ] 03-02-RESEARCH.md → 03-02-FINDINGS.md: Graph Database Feasibility - Backlinks/graph view requirements, library options
- [ ] 03-03-RESEARCH.md → 03-03-FINDINGS.md: Local AI Models - On-device processing feasibility, model selection
- [ ] 03-04-RESEARCH.md → 03-04-FINDINGS.md: CRDT Library Selection - Future collaboration requirements, library trade-offs

#### Phase 4: P4 Feature Planning
**Goal**: Detailed planning for 5 long-term features with architecture, milestones, and dependencies documented
**Depends on**: Phase 3 (research findings inform P4 plans)
**Plans**: 5 plans (one per P4 feature)

Plans:
- [ ] 04-01-PLAN.md: Real-time Collaboration Planning - Yjs integration, conflict resolution, sync architecture
- [ ] 04-02-PLAN.md: Plugin System Planning - API design, sandbox security, discovery/distribution
- [ ] 04-03-PLAN.md: Mobile Apps Planning - React Native vs native, sync strategy, offline-first
- [ ] 04-04-PLAN.md: Publishing Mode Planning - Static export, privacy controls, theming
- [ ] 04-05-PLAN.md: Advanced Automation Planning - Service Worker setup, trigger expansion, action library

## Progress

| Phase | Milestone | Plans Complete | Status | Completed |
|-------|-----------|----------------|--------|-----------|
| v1.0 Foundation | v1.0 | P0-P3 | Complete | 2025-11-30 |
| 1. Quality & Refinement | v1.1 | 3/3 | Complete | 2025-12-01 |
| 2. Quick Wins | v1.1 | 5/9 | In progress | - |
| 3. Architecture Research | v1.2 | 0/4 | Not started | - |
| 4. P4 Feature Planning | v1.2 | 0/5 | Not started | - |

## Strategic Notes

**Dependency Rationale:**
- Phase 1 before Phase 2: Quality foundation prevents quick wins from introducing regressions
- Phase 2 before Phase 3: Real usage patterns from quick wins inform architecture research
- Phase 3 before Phase 4: Research findings directly guide P4 feature planning (e.g., CRDT choice affects collaboration plan)

**Value Delivery:**
- v1.1 (Phases 1-2): Immediate user value - production-ready platform + 5 new features
- v1.2 (Phases 3-4): Strategic value - informed decisions for long-term roadmap

**Execution Strategy:**
- Phases 1-2: Execute via `/run-plan` for surgical, focused delivery
- Phase 3: Use RESEARCH.md prompts → FINDINGS.md outputs for systematic evaluation
- Phase 4: Create detailed PLAN.md files for future execution (not immediate implementation)

# Roadmap: Neuman Brain Post-P0-P3

## Overview

Transform Neuman Brain from solid foundation (P0-P3 complete) to competitive all-in-one platform through systematic quality refinement, quick-win features, strategic architecture research, and P4 planning. Four-phase approach optimizes for user value delivery (quality + quick wins first) while building knowledge base for future features (research + P4 planning).

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 Foundation** - P0-P3 complete (shipped 2025-11-30)
- 🚧 **v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins** - Phases 1-2 (in progress)
- 📋 **v1.2 Architecture Foundation** - Phases 3-4 (planned)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 Foundation (P0-P3) - SHIPPED 2025-11-30</summary>

**Completed Work:**
- P0: Core features (Tasks, Notes, Calendar, Time Tracking, Dashboard)
- P1: Advanced features (Forms, Graph View, Daily Notes, Automation)
- P2: Polish (Gantt export, Recurrence UI, Tag enhancements, Analytics)
- P3: Advanced recurrence patterns

**Outcome:**
- 89,472 lines of TypeScript/React
- 128/128 tests passing
- 117KB gzip bundle size
- Local-first architecture proven
- Zero telemetry, E2EE backups working

</details>

### 🚧 v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins (In Progress)

**Milestone Goal:** Deliver production-ready platform with enhanced UX and high-value quick-win features.

#### Phase 1: Quality & Refinement
**Goal**: Harden platform for production with comprehensive testing, technical debt resolution, and user documentation
**Depends on**: v1.0 foundation complete
**Plans**: 3 plans (E2E tests, technical debt, documentation/UX)

Plans:
- [x] 01-01-PLAN.md: E2E Test Coverage - Playwright tests for all critical user flows (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-02-PLAN.md: Technical Debt Resolution - IndexedDB optimization, full-text search, bundle analysis (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-03-PLAN.md: Documentation & UX Polish - User docs, onboarding, UI refinements (completed 2025-12-01)

#### Phase 2: Quick Wins
**Goal**: Ship 6 high-value features that enhance daily usage without architectural complexity
**Depends on**: Phase 1 (quality foundation ensures quick wins don't break)
**Plans**: 9 plans (features split for quality, optimal execution order)

Plans:
- [x] 02-01-PLAN.md: Note Tags - Hashtag syntax, tag filtering, tag management UI (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-02-PLAN.md: Note Templates Backend - Template CRUD, variable substitution, validation (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-03-PLAN.md: Note Templates UI - Template library, picker integration, settings (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-04-PLAN.md: Natural Language Task Dates - Parser & Component (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-05-PLAN.md: Natural Language Date UI Integration - QuickAddModal, CardDetailPanel, keyboard shortcuts (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-06-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - Core Utilities (Lexical → MD, Task → MD, ZIP) (completed 2025-12-01)
- [ ] 02-07-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - UI Integration (Notes/Tasks/Settings export UI)
- [ ] 02-08-PLAN.md: Help & Support System - Diagnostic reporting, mailto integration, footer/settings
- [ ] 02-09-PLAN.md: Task Completion Celebrations - Animations, sound effects, achievement tracking

### 📋 v1.2 Architecture Foundation (Planned)

**Milestone Goal:** Establish architectural decisions and research findings to guide P4 feature development.

#### Phase 3: Architecture Research
**Goal**: Systematic evaluation of 4 architectural decisions with evidence, trade-offs, and recommendations
**Depends on**: Phase 2 (quick wins provide real usage data to inform architecture)
**Plans**: 4 plans (one per research domain)

Plans:
- [ ] 03-01-RESEARCH.md → 03-01-FINDINGS.md: Block Editor Evaluation - Notion/Obsidian approaches, library comparison
- [ ] 03-02-RESEARCH.md → 03-02-FINDINGS.md: Graph Database Feasibility - Backlinks/graph view requirements, library options
- [ ] 03-03-RESEARCH.md → 03-03-FINDINGS.md: Local AI Models - On-device processing feasibility, model selection
- [ ] 03-04-RESEARCH.md → 03-04-FINDINGS.md: CRDT Library Selection - Future collaboration requirements, library trade-offs

#### Phase 4: P4 Feature Planning
**Goal**: Detailed planning for 5 long-term features with architecture, milestones, and dependencies documented
**Depends on**: Phase 3 (research findings inform P4 plans)
**Plans**: 5 plans (one per P4 feature)

Plans:
- [ ] 04-01-PLAN.md: Real-time Collaboration Planning - Yjs integration, conflict resolution, sync architecture
- [ ] 04-02-PLAN.md: Plugin System Planning - API design, sandbox security, discovery/distribution
- [ ] 04-03-PLAN.md: Mobile Apps Planning - React Native vs native, sync strategy, offline-first
- [ ] 04-04-PLAN.md: Publishing Mode Planning - Static export, privacy controls, theming
- [ ] 04-05-PLAN.md: Advanced Automation Planning - Service Worker setup, trigger expansion, action library

## Progress

| Phase | Milestone | Plans Complete | Status | Completed |
|-------|-----------|----------------|--------|-----------|
| v1.0 Foundation | v1.0 | P0-P3 | Complete | 2025-11-30 |
| 1. Quality & Refinement | v1.1 | 3/3 | Complete | 2025-12-01 |
| 2. Quick Wins | v1.1 | 5/9 | In progress | - |
| 3. Architecture Research | v1.2 | 0/4 | Not started | - |
| 4. P4 Feature Planning | v1.2 | 0/5 | Not started | - |

## Strategic Notes

**Dependency Rationale:**
- Phase 1 before Phase 2: Quality foundation prevents quick wins from introducing regressions
- Phase 2 before Phase 3: Real usage patterns from quick wins inform architecture research
- Phase 3 before Phase 4: Research findings directly guide P4 feature planning (e.g., CRDT choice affects collaboration plan)

**Value Delivery:**
- v1.1 (Phases 1-2): Immediate user value - production-ready platform + 5 new features
- v1.2 (Phases 3-4): Strategic value - informed decisions for long-term roadmap

**Execution Strategy:**
- Phases 1-2: Execute via `/run-plan` for surgical, focused delivery
- Phase 3: Use RESEARCH.md prompts → FINDINGS.md outputs for systematic evaluation
- Phase 4: Create detailed PLAN.md files for future execution (not immediate implementation)
